title: Roaring for Tigers Around the World
story: Tigers get their own special day every July 29 when people around the world celebrate International Tiger Day! Zoos host tiger talks and feeding shows, while wildlife groups share amazing tiger photos and facts on social media. Many people adopt tigers symbolically or donate to tiger rescue centers. Some families even have tiger movie marathons or bake orange cupcakes with black stripes to join the celebration!

activity_choices: Protecting wild tigers and their habitats
activity_answer: Protecting wild tigers and their habitats

title: Japan Opens Its Doors to the World
story: Imagine a country closed off from the rest of the world for centuries. That was Japan until 1858. On July 29 that year, Japan and the United States signed the Treaty of Amity and Commerce, also called the Harris Treaty. This important agreement opened Japanese ports to trade with America, marking the end of Japan's long isolation and the beginning of exciting new connections.
